<current_datetime>2026-05-12T15:52:52.415Z</current_datetime>

I have this MSTest data-driven test using DynamicData with object arrays. Is there a more type-safe way to write this in modern MSTest?

```csharp
[TestMethod]
[DynamicData(nameof(TestData))]
public void Validate_ReturnsExpected(string input, bool expected)
{
    var result = Validator.IsValid(input);
    Assert.AreEqual(expected, result);
}

public static IEnumerable<object[]> TestData =>
[
    ["valid@email.com", true],
    ["invalid", false],
    ["", false],
];
```

data.reasoningText: The user is asking about a more type-safe way to write MSTest data-driven tests using `DynamicData`. In modern MSTest (v3+), you can use `ValueTuple` instead of `object[]` for type-safe dynamic data. Let me provide the answer directly since this is a knowledge question.
